To our brothers and sisters in the United States, especially those within the Christian community who may feel uncertain about the current peace walks by Buddhist monks: We want to sincerely apologize if these actions have caused any discomfort or concern. Please be assured that there is absolutely no threat to any individual, group, or religion. Our faith is rooted in the principles of Metta (Loving-kindness) and Karuna (Compassion). We are taught to treat every human being as a family member and to never harbor ill will, even toward those who oppose us. The monks walking through your streets are not there to protest, convert, or cause disruption. They walk because the world is in deep pain. I am writing to you from Myanmar, where we are witnessing a tragic humanitarian crisis—lives are being lost, homes are being burned, and families are being torn apart by violence every single day. These monks carry that pain with them, representing not only the suffering people of Myanmar but every nation currently enduring the hardships of war and injustice. In Buddhism, we believe that peace begins with a quiet heart and a kind step. This walk is a prayer in motion—a call for global healing and a wish for all beings, regardless of their faith, to live free from fear and oppression. We share many of the same values you hold dear: love for our neighbors and a desire for a world defined by peace rather than conflict. We ask for your understanding and your prayers. Let us stand together in the shared hope that peace will prevail over violence, both in Myanmar and across the entire world😢😢
